118663.explanation: Quality is paramount. Given equal suitability, there is a preference for an equal male-female ratio.

118664.explanation: A referendum can involve residents effectively in important decisions. This only works if it is carefully organized and the result is actually taken into account.

118666.explanation: Increasing fees is not a goal in itself. However, permits must be as cost-covering as possible so that the municipality remains financially healthy.

118668.explanation: Citizen Forum supports local energy cooperatives and backs initiatives that want to invest in solutions themselves, alongside existing subsidy options.

118670.explanation: Employers must arrange proper housing for seasonal workers themselves, so this does not come at the expense of housing stock for our residents.

118674.explanation: Carbide shooting is part of our intangible heritage and we have also recorded this in our Environmental Vision.

118675.explanation: There is no such thing as free waste disposal. The costs are always passed on through the waste levy. However, we do find that Losser should maintain a fully-fledged waste collection point.

118678.explanation: We do not exclude this possibility, as long as location and surroundings are decisive and it aligns with our growth ambition.

118679.explanation: A strong, independent local broadcaster is essential for democracy and local engagement. That is why we consider extra support important.

118685.explanation: BOAs (special enforcement officers) only make sense if they are deployed effectively and purposefully.

118686.explanation: Camera surveillance can help, for example, to prevent waste dumping and increase the sense of security.

118687.explanation: This is arranged regionally. The scale of the municipality of Losser is too small to organize this independently.

118690.explanation: Citizen Forum is in favor of exploring as many options as possible to tackle the housing shortage. It must fit within our Environmental Vision and growth ambition.

118694.explanation: We have already raised the income limit in this council period. We continue to focus on support, especially for families with children.

118697.explanation: In certain places and within the center, yes, but throughout the entire built-up area it is not useful or feasible, because the roads would also have to be adjusted.

118698.explanation: Care must be equal for everyone, without distinction of culture or belief.

118702.explanation: Expansion appears desirable and necessary after research, combined with revitalization of business parks.

118704.explanation: Priority for permit holders is not fair to housing seekers from our own municipality.

118706.explanation: We invest in art, culture, and social encounters, among other things through the development of the Aloysius location.

118708.explanation: You often get that at home and what is truly seen as Twents often differs per village.

118711.explanation: We want to allow this (including renting a room in someone’s home) but under strict conditions.

118713.explanation: The current number must be maintained and stores must remain easily accessible.

118714.explanation: A completely car-free center would be harmful to the businesses in our center.

118715.explanation: A permanent asylum seekers' center is not a local starting point and depends on national policy. We comply with legal obligations but will hold off for now.

118716.explanation: Our fight against wind turbines continues, including against wind projects on German territory right at our border.

118717.explanation: New construction must respect the characteristic Old Tower and not cause it to disappear from view.

118663.explanation: We would like to equally divide men and women, but suitability is most important.

118664.explanation: A referendum is good, as long as the question is clear and everyone is involved.

118666.explanation: Permits should not cost more than necessary; it's better to reduce regulations.

118668.explanation: Local sustainable energy projects deserve support.

118670.explanation: Temporary housing on one’s own property can be allowed if it fits well.

118674.explanation: We want to collaborate on small-scale fireworks shows per village; traditions should be encouraged.

118675.explanation: Free collection of bulky waste costs the community money instead of the polluter. Free does not exist.

118678.explanation: There are limited possibilities for building on farmland; soil sealing must remain limited.

118679.explanation: Local broadcasting is important, but reach also counts.

118685.explanation: Losser is safe; more cameras can help before we hire extra special enforcement officers.

118686.explanation: Cameras are good if they genuinely help.

118687.explanation: There must be shelter for people fleeing this kind of violence when it occurs here.

118690.explanation: Holiday homes are temporary; in specific cases permanent residence is allowed.

118694.explanation: Everyone must be able to live with dignity; tailored solutions remain necessary.

118697.explanation: Reducing speed is good, except on main roads.

118698.explanation: Healthcare must be good; in specific/extreme cases, deployment of these workers will be coordinated with faith/culture.

118702.explanation: New business parks are needed due to shortage.

118704.explanation: Status holders do not have more rights than local residents; sometimes someone is given priority.

118706.explanation: Culture is important; first see what already exists before building something new. A new cultural center can therefore also be located on an existing site.

118708.explanation: Twents is culture and must be supported.

118711.explanation: We do not think renting rooms is a good idea; living is more than just a room.

118713.explanation: Research must determine if more parking spaces are really needed.

118714.explanation: The center must remain accessible; through traffic can be banned.

118715.explanation: We want shelter for people with a residence status. Asylum seeker accommodation preferably takes place on a small scale and is spread throughout the municipality.

118716.explanation: No large wind turbines, possibly small farm turbines.

118717.explanation: New construction must fit the environment and meet the criteria of good spatial planning. It must comply with applicable standards. Changing the rules during the process is not allowed.

118663.explanation: D66 considers diversity important but the quality of leaders must come first.

118664.explanation: Residents can already participate in many different ways on issues that concern them. For important topics, a referendum is often too limited. D66 wants to better define the role of stakeholders in decision-making so everyone knows where they stand.

118666.explanation: D66 is in favor of fees that are as cost-covering as possible.

118668.explanation: Further sustainability and local energy generation should be encouraged. The municipality can play an important role in this.

118670.explanation: If there are better alternatives, choose those. This also prevents cluttering of the countryside.

118674.explanation: Given the population size and the distribution of residents over the countryside and (small) villages, a fireworks show in one particular village will be visited by only a limited number of people.

118675.explanation: There is no such thing as free waste disposal. This would mean that waste fees would also increase for people who do not produce waste.

118678.explanation: The existing regulations already allow this sufficiently.

118679.explanation: RTV Losser already receives subsidies from the municipality to be able to perform their work properly.

118685.explanation: For safety and supervision in both the villages and the rural area. However, we must be careful not to take on tasks that the national government shifts off.

118686.explanation: In practice, camera surveillance does not lead to less nuisance.

118687.explanation: For safety and supervision in both the villages and the rural area. However, we must be careful not to take on tasks that the national government shifts off.

118690.explanation: This does not solve the current housing shortage and not all homes are suitable for permanent residence. Moreover, this comes at the expense of the recreation sector.

118694.explanation: Losser already applies a more generous limit than many other municipalities.

118697.explanation: Not on through roads in the center. Outside that, in neighborhoods, yes. This is already largely the case in the villages of Losser.

118698.explanation: Everyone has the right to equal care, regardless of faith, culture or belief. If you want differently, you can choose and pay for that.

118702.explanation: A limited expansion based on proven need. In addition, we must also carefully consider how to improve existing business parks.

118704.explanation: The municipality must carry out the legal tasks related to housing special target groups (including status holders).

118706.explanation: Only if it can be cost-covering, the need is great enough among associations and clubs, and the center does not empty other cultural centers because associations and clubs move to the new center.

118708.explanation: Fine to offer it but it must not become mandatory.

118711.explanation: Rental by housing corporations is preferred because they are legally required to make performance agreements with municipalities. There is limited oversight on private room rental, which can encourage abuses.

118713.explanation: The number of parking spaces must meet (future) demand.

118714.explanation: The center of Losser may be low-traffic but not car-free.

118715.explanation: The distribution law is a good solution to fairly allocate the required shelter capacity. Losser must contribute its share accordingly.

118716.explanation: We contribute to a sustainable solution for wind energy at a location that causes the least nuisance.

118717.explanation: A lower apartment complex may make construction unfeasible. So yes if possible, and no if not feasible.

118663.explanation: What matters is delivering quality, and whether that is done by a man or woman is less important to us.

118664.explanation: It is important that residents can have a say. We see participation as a replacement for a referendum. Participation already takes place but, in our view, it can be used better.

118666.explanation: If permits are not cost-covering, it should be examined whether some parts can be increased. This should, however, happen gradually.

118668.explanation: Sustainability is important for everyone, and those who cannot afford it should be able to receive financial support.

118670.explanation: The responsibility primarily lies with the employer of the seasonal worker.

118674.explanation: Now that there is a nationwide fireworks ban, the municipality must offer an alternative.

118675.explanation: In our view, the polluter pays, including for the collection of bulky waste.

118678.explanation: The farmer owns land and we consider housing important, including in rural areas. Think of red-for-red schemes or developing generational farms. The farmer should be allowed to develop this themselves.

118679.explanation: Local broadcasters are important but primarily the responsibility lies with The Hague. Occasionally, the municipality can see where it can assist.

118685.explanation: We focus on prevention, not repression.

118686.explanation: This creates the impression that cameras should be installed in many locations for safety reasons. However, this provides a false sense of security. For certain spots related to events, we are in favor. Additionally, we support more active controls and preventive measures.

118687.explanation: This is a task for a central municipality like Enschede. We will need to participate in this but will not establish our own domestic violence shelter.

118690.explanation: Holiday homes are not regular homes and often do not meet the requirements of the building code (safety).

118694.explanation: It is important that everyone can take part in our society, sustain themselves financially, and feel supported in doing so.

118697.explanation: This improves traffic safety within the towns, and we are strong supporters of that.

118698.explanation: This unnecessarily complicates care provision. Organizing care is already difficult, and then choosing who can deliver care is unnecessarily bureaucratic.

118702.explanation: Economic development is important for Losser. This includes expansion of business parks so companies can grow or establish themselves anew.

118704.explanation: We have a quota and these people also have a right to housing. To achieve this, Losser must build more homes for everyone.

118706.explanation: This definitely needs to happen. Various cultural associations lack housing. Culture is an important part of our society, and therefore these associations should have good facilities to conduct their activities.

118708.explanation: Twents is part of our culture and we believe it should be preserved. So young people must come into contact with it and continue doing so.

118711.explanation: This can be a solution to the current housing shortage under certain conditions.

118713.explanation: There are plenty of parking spaces in and especially around the center.

118714.explanation: For traffic safety, this would be beneficial. Good consultation with entrepreneurs in the center will be necessary.

118715.explanation: We have this legal obligation and should not shy away from it.

118716.explanation: There is an existing situation; the competent authority is the province. The municipality plays a limited role. It is important that the municipality ensures local ownership so the population can benefit. With new developments, we will carefully weigh all interests.

118717.explanation: The current plan is adapted to the surroundings and the Oale Toarn remains clearly visible from a distance.

118663.explanation: Diversity is preferred, but not only in terms of gender. Ultimately, it’s about the right knowledge and a team that can work together. Each party should also be able to put forward their preferred alderman.

118664.explanation: The VVD is critical of referenda. Elected representatives are chosen to weigh interests and make decisions. Due to low turnout and because issues often cannot be reduced to simple yes/no questions, the VVD believes referenda do not necessarily contribute to better democracy.

118666.explanation: The VVD opposes a general increase. However, we know in Losser that some fees need to rise because they are far from cost-covering.

118668.explanation: Financial support or subsidies are not the first thing the VVD thinks of. The municipality can provide support in other ways that make realization easier.

118670.explanation: Employers are responsible for decent housing, preferably on their own grounds or nearby business parks. This should prevent employees from ending up in poor and unsafe living conditions through third parties.

118674.explanation: The municipality does not need to do this. Tax money can be used more wisely.

118675.explanation: The VVD believes the polluter should pay. Therefore, bulky waste collection should not be free.

118678.explanation: Yes, the VVD is positive about loosening rules to build on agricultural land if that helps realize housing. The owner should be able to decide to develop, provided it is done carefully and fits the environment.

118679.explanation: Residents have a right to good, independent local information via the media. The VVD considers that important. It is evident that this requires some financial support.

118685.explanation: Safety is one of the municipality’s core tasks. Public order officials must be able to perform sufficient supervision and enforcement so that nuisance, crime, undermining, and feelings of insecurity can be addressed.

118686.explanation: The VVD is positive about camera surveillance, but always purposeful, proportional, and intended to increase safety and prevent nuisance. Previously, we supported camera surveillance to, for example, tackle dumping at underground waste containers.

118687.explanation: This is well organized on a Twente-wide scale.

118690.explanation: The infrastructure and facilities are not designed for that. Moreover, different tax rules apply to recreational homes.

118694.explanation: There are more options to get help in a financially vulnerable position than raising the income limit. In Losser, assistance for the financially vulnerable is already very well arranged with various provisions. An additional increase is not necessary.

118697.explanation: In residential neighborhoods, the rule is already that 30 km/h applies. To combat speeding, the focus should be more on speed controls and enforcement. Emergency services experience hindrance from the 30 km/h limit and the associated infrastructure, causing delays. That is undesirable.

118698.explanation: For the VVD, freedom of choice is central. Care must be nearby, human-centered, and suited to everyone’s life.

118702.explanation: There is currently sufficient space for businesses. The space that is free or becomes available must primarily be used for housing construction.

118704.explanation: Affordable homes are primarily for starters, young people, and movers from within the municipality. Refugees with residence status will be helped in a targeted and temporary manner — the municipality must house status holders — but always with attention to our local housing market and in consultation with the housing corporation.

118706.explanation: The VVD wants the current supply to be looked at better and in a coherent manner. That offers sufficient possibilities. No new cultural facility should be established that competes with other amenities or requires the municipality to provide (much) extra funding.

118708.explanation: According to the VVD, good regular education based on basic skills is necessary. Attention to Twente language and culture is voluntary and additional, and should not be an extra mandatory burden for schools or parents.

118711.explanation: If this can relieve pressure on the housing market, room rental under good conditions and with control/enforcement of rules should be an option.

118713.explanation: The current number of parking spaces must be at least maintained, and expansion to additional spaces for charging electric cars must not come at the expense of the existing capacity.

118714.explanation: The VVD values its entrepreneurs in the center. Losser is a shopping village and to reach entrepreneurs, it must be possible to come into the center by car and park nearby.

118715.explanation: The VVD prefers small-scale temporary shelter. A permanent asylum seekers’ center (AZC) puts pressure on services and safety. Moreover, there is no obligation for a permanent AZC, but the municipality does have a duty to temporarily accommodate a number of status holders and refugees.

118716.explanation: The VVD does not want landscape-polluting tall wind turbines, especially as long as the health effects are unknown.

118717.explanation: The VVD would like a height that fits the appearance, but this must not come at the expense of the overall housing development in that location, which we consider truly necessary.

118663.explanation: Of course, we find it important that the government is a good reflection of society. That leads to the best decisions. Women are still judged differently than men. It is good to be aware of this when selecting candidates.

118664.explanation: It is good that residents can give their opinion on important issues that directly affect them through consultative referenda. This stimulates local democracy.

118668.explanation: It’s great if residents generate energy together. Good for the wallet, the environment, and social cohesion. Much experience has already been gained with local energy cooperatives. The municipality can share this knowledge with residents and actively support them.

118670.explanation: People who come here to work temporarily must be provided with decent accommodation. The municipality must focus more on enforcement. We want to prevent dependency and exploitation of workers.

118674.explanation: We envision a beautiful show with fireworks and lasers. Safe and cozy to watch together. The municipality must enforce the general fireworks ban.

118675.explanation: This is the easiest solution for litter on the street. This service is also important for the elderly and those in need with a limited network.

118678.explanation: When farmers want to build a home, for example for their parents to be able to continue living on the farmyard, that is a charming solution for everyone. This may be on a building plot, not on agricultural land. Through 'red-for-red' schemes, farmers can demolish a barn in exchange for a house.

118679.explanation: Local media are important and enjoyable. But more money is not always necessary. If needed, an appeal can be made to the municipality.

118685.explanation: The neighborhood police officer, as a familiar face and connector, can be the first deployed for safety. BOAs (special enforcement officers) are responsible for enforcement of municipal regulations. Additional efforts can be made there.

118686.explanation: We do not need Big Brother in Losser.

118687.explanation: First of all, we want more focus on removing the perpetrator from the home instead of the victims. If that is not possible, the insecurity often means shelter elsewhere, such as Enschede or Zwolle. Regional agreements have been made about this.

118690.explanation: For as long as necessary. Of course, we must ensure more affordable housing. For permanent use of holiday homes, some rules must be adjusted, including tax rules. Also, municipal supervision is needed to prevent abuse.

118694.explanation: Due to rising energy costs and increasing prices, it has become harder to make ends meet. The municipality must be able to help if necessary, even if a family's income is a bit above the income limit. This requires a tailored approach.

118697.explanation: For cyclists and people with limited mobility, 30 km/h makes a huge difference. Fewer accidents, easier crossing, cleaner air.

118698.explanation: Unfortunately, this is not feasible given the procurement of care provisions by municipalities. Too many providers also lead to insufficient quality supervision. People with a personal budget (PGB) can buy care themselves.

118702.explanation: This can be done on a modest scale, as far as it fits within the ambitions and development plans of the municipality.

118704.explanation: Nationally, this has been dropped. There are other groups for whom urgency applies; we should not exclude them.

118708.explanation: It is nice to keep the Twente language, culture, and history alive. It is good to pay some attention to this in primary schools. This must not come at the expense of teaching in Dutch but should be a valuable addition.

118711.explanation: This way, young people who want to leave home can make a start. The municipality must supervise safety. More enforcement efforts are needed for this.

118713.explanation: A few more bicycle parking spots are allowed. When the parking pressure for cars in the center is high, there is still enough space at the Fakkel. A few additional spots for disabled people could be reserved in the center so that they always have a place nearby.

118714.explanation: GroenLinks wants an attractive and cozy center. Closing off parts to car traffic can be a way to create space. Then squares like those around the Martinus tower and in front of the Lossers Hoes can be better furnished with terraces, more greenery, and better visibility of our characteristic buildings.

118715.explanation: An asylum seekers’ center (AZC) is in principle temporary. When the COA (Central Agency for the Reception of Asylum Seekers) calls on our municipality, we will consider a suitable location. It is important to take into account the livability and safety of everyone, including the refugees.

118716.explanation: Generating enough sustainable energy makes us future-proof. Cooperation also means thinking along about the best location. The proceeds must benefit the residents.

118717.explanation: We want to solve the housing shortage.

statements.title: The new apartment buildings around the Oale Toarn in Losser should be lower than planned.
Losser should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The business parks in Losser should be allowed to expand.
The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Losser.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The prices of municipal permits must be raised.

titleParts.glossary: Permission from the municipality to do something.

statements.title: Losser should have a permanent asylum seekers' center (AZC).

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to include attention to Twents.
There should be more parking spaces in the center of Losser.
The residents of Losser should be able to express their opinions on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Losser moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
Losser should have a new cultural center.
Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The center of Losser should become car-free.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: Losser should have a women's shelter (a refuge for victims of domestic violence).

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: Losser should cooperate with the installation of wind turbines.

titleParts.text: Losser moet meewerken aan de plaatsing van

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.
